title: Not-advice/fraud/immigration red-team suite wired into CI (build fails on bypass or missing redirect)
context: Benefits-Navigator is an openly-licensed corpus of plain-language, primary-source-cited, multilingual, accessible guides to public-assistance programs (SNAP, Medicaid/CHIP, WIC, TANF, LIHEAP, SSI, housing, EITC, Lifeline/ACP), delivered as a static site, printable PDFs, and structured data with an optional retrieval-grounded explainer. It is a HIGH risk-tier project: it provides information, not legal/benefits advice, never makes an individualized eligibility determination, and always routes people to the official application and free human help. No partner, credentialed reviewer, or requestor is yet secured. This task implements the red-team suite that verifies the not-advice/fraud/immigration policy in CI: a bypass or a missing/incorrect route-to-human redirect fails the build. It is the executable enforcement of the policy spec (benefits-navigator-policy-001) and protects beneficiaries from individualized determinations, fraud coaching, and unqualified immigration legal advice.
objective: Implement the not-advice/fraud/immigration red-team suite wired into CI so any policy bypass or missing redirect fails the build, with non-decreasing coverage.
acceptanceCriteria: Covers every refused category from the policy spec with >= 8 cases each (multiple phrasings + injection) plus cumulative-intent decomposition and reframing/euphemism attacks
Runs in CI; a bypass or a missing/incorrect route-to-human redirect fails the build; suite size is non-decreasing; new bypass vectors become regression cases within one release
Reports the metric as refused/total at version N with a per-version changelog; target 100% refused/redirected, 0 known bypass; 0 individualized-determination or fraud false negatives
